patch.value: Declarative extension authoring: composeExtension blueprint + deriveExtensionCapabilities + defineExtensionManifest

Capstone of the SDK authoring tripod (author -> register -> test). Today every package author hand-wires each api.register* call inside an imperative activate(api) and hand-syncs manifest.capabilities, which is the two biggest package-authoring footguns (forgotten/mis-ordered registration; over- or under-declared capabilities). This adds a declarative authoring surface so an author (or agent) describes WHAT to register as data and the SDK generates the correct activate:

1. composeExtension(blueprint): ExtensionModule — accepts arrays/records of define*-authored definitions (commands, commandOverrides, flags, parsers, renderers, services, preflights, itemTypes, itemFields, migrations, searchProviders, vectorStoreAdapters, importers, exporters, the 5 hook kinds) plus optional manifest/activate/deactivate, and returns a module whose generated activate calls every api.register* in a deterministic order, then runs any explicit imperative activate. Pure assembler; validation stays in register* (ADR pm-3mph).
2. deriveExtensionCapabilities(blueprint): ExtensionCapability[] — author-time inverse of reconcileExtensionCapabilityUsage (pm-fk84): computes the minimal least-privilege capability set a blueprint exercises, so manifest.json capabilities can be authored exactly with no declared-but-unused / used-but-undeclared drift. Mapping mirrors EXTENSION_CAPABILITY_REGISTRATION_SURFACES (flags/itemTypes/itemFields/migrations -> schema, etc).
3. defineExtensionManifest(manifest): ExtensionManifest — completes the define* identity family (the manifest was the one authoring surface without a builder); types the in-module manifest mirror.

Architecture: move ExtensionModule + defineExtension into the new src/sdk/compose.ts (no import cycle; re-exported from the barrel so the public API is unchanged). NOT dogfooded into the bundled first-party packages because those are intentionally import-free for extension-only installs; demonstrated in docs/SDK.md + tests instead, targeting npm package-mode authors who depend on the SDK. 100% test + docstring coverage; SDK.md Declarative Authoring section.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: deriveExtensionCapabilities and composeExtension agree with the loader BY CONSTRUCTION: the field->capability table mirrors EXTENSION_CAPABILITY_REGISTRATION_SURFACES (registerFlags/registerItemFields/registerItemTypes/registerMigration all -> schema, NOT commands; registerImporter/registerExporter -> importers; search providers + vector adapters -> search). Verified end-to-end: a full blueprint activated with exactly its derived capabilities produces zero capability-missing failures. composeExtension is a pure assembler with no validation (ADR pm-3mph) - per-surface contract enforcement stays in api.register*. defineExtension/ExtensionModule were relocated from index.ts into compose.ts (type-only ExtensionCapability import from loader.js, no import cycle) and re-exported from the barrel, so the public API is unchanged. The bundled first-party packages deliberately keep import-free hand-written activate bodies (so they load in extension-only installs without SDK module resolution), so composeExtension is documented for npm package-mode authors, NOT dogfooded into the bundled exemplars.

patch.value: Delivered composeExtension + deriveExtensionCapabilities + defineExtensionManifest as the declarative-authoring capstone of the SDK author->register->test loop; 100% coverage/docstrings, all gates green, smoke-verified through the built bundle and the real pm CLI.

Shipped declarative extension authoring on branch feat/sdk-declarative-compose-extension. New src/sdk/compose.ts exports composeExtension(blueprint)->ExtensionModule (generates an activate that wires every populated surface via api.register* in deterministic order, then awaits an optional imperative activate escape hatch), deriveExtensionCapabilities(blueprint)->least-privilege capability set, and the relocated ExtensionModule + defineExtension (re-exported from the barrel; public API unchanged). define.ts gains defineExtensionManifest, completing the define* identity family. docs/SDK.md adds a Declarative Authoring section. Verified: full suite 4872 tests 100% coverage (stmts/branch/funcs/lines), typecheck (both tsconfigs) clean, static + docs-skills + contracts gates green. Manual temp-dir smoke: built-bundle E2E (deriveExtensionCapabilities->[commands,schema], activation 0 failures with exactly the derived caps, command dispatched) AND real pm CLI subprocess (extension loaded from <pmRoot>/extensions, pm extension describe reflects it, pm <cmd> dispatched, pm package doctor clean activation with 0 capability/consistency warnings). Not dogfooded into bundled packages (they stay import-free for extension-only installs).

Package authors (and agents) can author an extension declaratively: composeExtension(blueprint) generates the activate that wires every api.register* surface, deriveExtensionCapabilities(blueprint) yields the exact least-privilege manifest capability set, and defineExtensionManifest types the manifest mirror. Public SDK API unchanged; 100% coverage + docstrings; all gates green.

Delivered exactly that. composeExtension + deriveExtensionCapabilities (src/sdk/compose.ts) + defineExtensionManifest (src/sdk/define.ts), ExtensionModule/defineExtension relocated and re-exported from the barrel. 4872 tests at 100% coverage, typecheck/static/docs/contracts gates green, and verified end-to-end through both the built bundle and a real pm CLI subprocess (extension loaded, described, dispatched; package doctor clean).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: PR #346 review round 1 — addressed and pushed. Gemini (4 findings, all the same REAL null-robustness theme): composeExtension and deriveExtensionCapabilities now treat an explicit null (optional field or hooks) like undefined instead of throwing (Object.keys(null) / null-hooks deref) — hasEntries uses !value, hooks use `?? {}` (Gemini's literal `hooks !== null` suggestion would not compile since null is not in the type). Added an explicit-null robustness unit test. CodeRabbit (2 findings): (1) REAL docstring over-claim — defineExtensionManifest does NOT preserve a `capabilities` tuple literal (ExtensionManifest.capabilities is string[]; only `as const` would), reworded define.ts + SDK.md to the accurate contract-checking value; (2) trivial SDK.md `exactly` repetition reworded. Sourcery rate-limited (weekly cap); Codex not connected. Re-verified: 4872 tests 100% coverage, typecheck/static/docs gates green. Re-triggering bots for round 2.
